title: Review: wolftpm crate suite (Device, EccKey, TSS Connection)
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — duplicate swtpm init is the primary concern; it is a maintenance-time bomb not a runtime bug. The Send impl is a structural risk but wolfTPM is stable and well-tested.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: unsafe impl Send for Device (depends on wolfTPM internal thread safety guarantees I cannot verify from this repo). Uncertainty driven by: C library internals not visible; FMT1 modifier bit logic relies on TPM2 spec interpretation. Would raise confidence if: wolfTPM source tree reviewed for global state; FMT2 variant codes (with modifier combinations) were tested against an actual TPM.
Idiom rating: CLEAN — error types are structured and non-exhaustive, all unsafe blocks have SAFETY comments, borrows are correctly scoped, no gratuitous clone, iterator patterns are used correctly. The only idiomatic concern is the duplicate helpers across crates which is a structural consequence of the crate split, not a Rust idiom failure.
Rust idiom confidence: 10% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: e.get() as u32 cast in wolftpm-tss/src/error.rs:126 (depends on TssError::get() return type from unpublished tpm-rs crate). Uncertainty driven by: tpm-rs git dep not in local checkout.
